title: Day 36: O3 Consolidation Continues; Argentina CJNG FTO Designation Takes Effect; Operativo Enjambre Sweeps Expand
detail: Day 36 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Succession timeline to date: El Tuli (financial/logistics chief) killed Feb 22; El Doble R (senior operations commander) arrested March 11; El Pepé (key logistics link) arrested March 16; Tequila, Jalisco municipal president arrested March 29 in Operativo Enjambre. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González), El Mencho's U.S.-born stepson, continues consolidating leadership under a collective council structure per U.S. NCTC and Wall Street Journal reporting. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) — the primary remaining high-value target carrying a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty — continues operating as an independent regional power center controlling plazas in Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, and Colima. Argentina's March 26 FTO designation of CJNG — the third such designation after the U.S. (February 2025) and Canada — represents a growing multilateral legal framework targeting the cartel's international operations across 40+ countries. SEDENA and National Guard continue high-tempo operations across Jalisco, Michoacán, and Colima under the post-Tapalpa pressure campaign. No new senior-command-level captures publicly confirmed for Day 36.

title: Operativo Enjambre: Dozens of CJNG-Linked Officials Arrested Across Jalisco and Michoacán Since Feb 22
detail: One month and eight days into the post-Tapalpa pressure campaign, Operativo Enjambre has produced a documented pattern of institutional-capture dismantlement across CJNG's territorial footprint. Key arrests in sequence: five Chavinda, Michoacán municipal officials (March 19–20), including síndica Ana Laura Covarrubias Ramos (classified a CJNG 'priority operator'), two security directors, and two regidores — with seized items including a bulletproof vest bearing CJNG insignia, assault rifles, and cocaine; Tequila, Jalisco municipal president (March 29), charged with extortion, homicide facilitation, and functioning as a CJNG liaison official. Security analysts at WOLA and InSight Crime note that dismantling local government corruption networks is essential to preventing plaza bosses from reconstituting operational capacity following national-level leadership decapitation — the 'narco-administración' model that has allowed CJNG to operate quasi-openly in Jalisco for over a decade. The Tequila arrest is symbolically significant: the internationally known distilling town sits in the highland Jalisco zone that formed CJNG's historical operational core. Operativo Enjambre has resulted in dozens of public official arrests across CJNG's primary zones since February 22, constituting one of Mexico's most sustained anti-corruption sweeps targeting cartel-infiltrated municipal governments.
